Summer holly is a handsome manzanita relative of southern California chaparral, with leathery leaves, urn-shaped white flowers, and warty red berries for birds. A choice evergreen for dry gardens.
Shrubby cinquefoil is a tough, compact shrub covered in butter-yellow flowers all summer, thriving from alpine meadows to tough urban sites. Cold-hardy, drought-tolerant, and deer-resistant.
Island bush poppy is an evergreen California shrub smothered for months in satiny golden poppy flowers over blue-gray leaves. Fast and drought-proof on well-drained, sunny sites.
Santa Cruz Island buckwheat is a mounding evergreen shrub topped for months with flat pink-and-cream flower clusters that pollinators swarm, drying to rust. A superb, drought-proof butterfly plant.
Red-flowered buckwheat is a compact California island buckwheat with rose-pink pompom flowers over gray-green rosettes, a long-blooming, drought-hardy pollinator gem for sunny banks.
Desert trumpet is a curious desert buckwheat whose hollow, swollen flower stems balloon out below airy yellow flower sprays. A drought-adapted oddity for hot, sandy gardens.
Redroot buckwheat sends up tall wands of white-to-pink flowers over basal rosettes in dry western woodlands and slopes, a late-summer nectar source that ages to russet.

Mexican flannelbush is a rare, fast evergreen shrub blazing with large waxy golden-orange flowers in spring, spectacular and drought-proof on dry, well-drained slopes. Handle leaves with care (irritating hairs).
Wright's silktassel is a drought-hardy Southwestern evergreen shrub draped with dangling greenish catkins, followed by purple berries for birds. A tough, deer-resistant screen for dry sites.
Broom snakeweed is a low, rounded subshrub that turns golden in late summer with tiny massed flowers, a tough, drought-proof pollinator plant of dry western rangelands.
Rock spirea (mountain spray) is a graceful western shrub draped with creamy, pink-tinged flower plumes in summer, drought-hardy on rocky slopes and lovely cascading over walls.
Island mallow is a fast, nearly ever-blooming California island shrub with rose-pink hibiscus-like flowers, salt- and drought-tolerant and excellent for quick coastal screening.
Mendocino bush mallow is a soft gray-leaved California shrub with spires of pink mallow flowers, a fast, drought-tolerant, fire-following plant beloved by native bees.
Laurel sumac is a fast, rounded evergreen chaparral shrub with aromatic laurel-like leaves and creamy flower clusters, followed by small fruits for birds. Drought-proof and quick screening.

Tanoak is a stately evergreen oak relative of the Pacific Coast, with leathery leaves and acorns that were a staple food for Indigenous peoples and remain vital wildlife mast. (Susceptible to sudden oak death.)
Heath cliffrose is a fine-leaved evergreen Southwestern shrub with pale cream-yellow flowers, drought-hardy on dry limestone and an important browse plant for desert wildlife.
Mexican cliffrose is a large evergreen shrub of Southwestern uplands with fragrant cream flowers and feathery seed plumes, drought-proof and valuable browse and cover for wildlife.
Stansbury cliffrose is a fragrant, fragrant-flowered evergreen shrub of the Southwest, smothered in creamy-white blossoms in spring then silvery seed plumes. Drought-proof and key wildlife browse.
Prairie acacia is a thornless, ferny-leaved legume shrub with white puffball flowers, a nitrogen-fixing, drought-hardy native for hot, dry, rocky ground.
Desert honeysuckle is a Southwestern shrub with tubular orange-red flowers that hummingbirds chase, blooming after rains. Drought-tolerant and a larval host for the elada checkerspot.
Shadscale is a spiny, silvery saltbush of cold deserts and alkaline flats, vital browse and seed for wildlife and a tough soil-binder for saline, arid ground.
Desert holly is the silvery-white, holly-like saltbush of the hottest deserts, its reflective leaves shrugging off extreme heat and drought. A striking, salt-tolerant accent.
Anacacho orchid tree is a graceful multi-trunked Texas shrub with split butterfly-shaped leaves and clusters of white-to-pink orchid-like flowers in spring. Drought-tough and elegant.
Chiltepin is the wild, bird-dispersed ancestor of the chile pepper, a small shrub bearing tiny fiery-hot red fruit beloved by birds. Native to far southern Texas and Arizona.
Florida rosemary is an aromatic, needle-leaved evergreen shrub of Florida scrub and coastal dunes, a keystone of the rare rosemary-scrub ecosystem on deep, dry sand.
Texas snakewood is a stiff, intricately branched shrub of Texas brushlands with small greenish flowers and dark fruit for birds, exceptionally drought- and heat-tolerant.
Brasil (bluewood condalia) is a thorny evergreen shrub or small tree of south Texas, bearing sweet blue-black fruit relished by birds and people. Tough, drought-proof brushland cover.
Rosary babybonnets is an airy desert legume shrub flushed in spring with cream-pink pea flowers, a nitrogen-fixer and larval host that hosts cochineal-like insects valued by wildlife.
Ragged rockflower is a spiny desert-canyon shrub with fragrant, ragged-petaled white flowers in late winter, a drought-hardy native for rocky slopes and washes.
Sixangle foldwing is a sprawling coastal perennial with slender red-orange tubular flowers that hummingbirds love, blooming for months in sunny, sandy Southern gardens.
Hopbush is a fast, glossy-leaved evergreen shrub with showy papery pink-tan seed capsules, drought- and salt-tolerant and excellent for quick screening in warm climates.
Texas ebony is a stately, dark-trunked evergreen legume tree of south Texas, with fragrant cream puffball flowers and a dense canopy. A premier drought-proof shade tree.
Southwestern coral bean is a dramatic desert shrub that opens spikes of brilliant scarlet tubular flowers before its leaves, drawing hummingbirds. Striking red seeds are toxic.
Texas kidneywood is an airy, fragrant legume shrub that flushes white flower spikes after rains, a nitrogen-fixer beloved by bees and butterflies and browsed by wildlife.
Cliff fendlerbush is a Southwestern rock-slope shrub smothered in fragrant white-to-pink four-petaled flowers in spring, drought-hardy and graceful for dry, rocky gardens.
Spiny greasebush is a stiff, intricately twiggy desert shrub with tiny white flowers, tucked into limestone cliffs and rocky slopes across the arid West.
Tenaza is a graceful, fine-leaved legume tree of south Texas with fragrant cream puffball flowers off and on through the warm season. A nitrogen-fixing, drought-proof small shade tree.
Coyotillo is a thornless brushland shrub of south Texas with glossy leaves and dark fruit; birds eat the fruit, though the seeds are toxic to mammals. Tough and drought-hardy.
Pitcher sage is an aromatic California chaparral subshrub with large white-to-lavender salvia-like flowers that hummingbirds and bees work, drought-proof for sunny dry slopes.
Island pitcher sage is a fragrant California shrub with soft gray-green leaves and lavender flowers, an aromatic, drought-tolerant pollinator plant for dry gardens.
Goldenball leadtree is a delicate, open Texas legume tree that bears bright golden puffball flowers after rains, a fast, nitrogen-fixing small shade tree for hot, dry sites.
Anderson's wolfberry is a spiny desert shrub with small lavender flowers and red berries that birds and mammals depend on, an early-blooming, drought-proof native of the Southwest.
Carolina wolfberry is a salt-tolerant coastal shrub with lavender flowers and red berries that are critical fall food for migrating whooping cranes and other birds.
Fragrant mimosa is a thorny, rounded shrub that perfumes the air with fluffy pink flower balls in spring, a drought-hardy nitrogen-fixer for rocky slopes.
Velvetpod mimosa is a prickly Southwestern shrub with bottlebrush spikes of rose-pink flowers in summer, a tough, nitrogen-fixing pollinator plant for dry uplands.
Texas mimosa (catclaw mimosa) is a dense, thorny brushland shrub with fragrant cream-pink puffballs, important nesting cover and a nitrogen-fixer for south Texas thornscrub.
Mexican palo verde (Jerusalem thorn) is a fast, green-barked desert tree with weeping branches and showers of yellow flowers, casting light shade with photosynthetic stems. Very drought-proof.
Texas palo verde is a green-barked, spiny large shrub or small tree of south Texas, glowing with yellow flowers in spring, a drought-proof nitrogen-fixer for hot, dry sites.
Mexican pistache is a graceful, compound-leaved small tree of the Texas-Mexico borderlands with brilliant fall color and fruit for birds, drought-hardy and underused as a shade tree.
Hoary rosemarymint is a silvery, aromatic mint shrub of sandy deserts with lavender flowers and edible, oregano-scented leaves, drought-proof for hot, sandy gardens.
Broom dalea is a wispy, broom-like desert shrub that hazes blue-purple with pea flowers after summer rains, a nitrogen-fixing pollinator plant for deep sand.
Smoke tree is a nearly leafless, gray, spiny desert tree that looks like drifting smoke until it bursts into indigo-blue flowers, a spectacular nitrogen-fixer of desert washes.
Western soapberry is a tough, drought- and cold-hardy native shade tree with airy white flower clusters, golden fall color, and translucent amber fruits once used as soap.
Desert yaupon is a dense, fine-textured evergreen shrub of south Texas brush with translucent red berries that birds love, a tough, drought-proof native for screening.
Wright's acacia (catclaw) is a thorny, fragrant legume tree of Texas and the Southwest whose cream flower spikes are a major bee plant, with seeds and cover for wildlife.
Necklace pod is a silvery-leaved coastal shrub with long spikes of yellow pea flowers nearly year-round, drawing hummingbirds and bees. Salt- and drought-tolerant; seeds are toxic.
Eve's necklace is a graceful small legume tree with drooping pink wisteria-like flowers in spring and distinctive beaded black seed pods, a refined, drought-tolerant native shade tree.
Mexican buckeye flushes fragrant rose-pink flowers on bare branches in early spring, a multi-trunked large shrub or small tree with good fall color. Drought-hardy; seeds are toxic.
Blackbrush acacia is a dense, thorny, dark-stemmed legume shrub of south Texas with fragrant cream flower spikes, a major bee plant and nesting cover in thornscrub.
Slimleaf rosewood is a leathery evergreen shrub or small tree of Texas-Mexico mountains with flat white flower clusters, drought-proof and handsome for screening on dry, rocky ground.
Florida hobblebush (pipestem) is a graceful evergreen shrub with arching, glossy-leaved stems and drooping clusters of white bell flowers, ideal for shaded, moist woodland edges in the Southeast.
Bog rosemary is a low, slender evergreen shrub of cold northern bogs, with blue-green needle-like leaves and nodding pink urn-shaped flowers. A choice plant for acidic, peaty wetland gardens.
Tarflower is a Florida scrub shrub with showy, sticky, fragrant white-to-pink flowers that trap small insects, a heat- and drought-tolerant evergreen for dry, sandy, acidic gardens.
Western sweetshrub (spice bush) is a California shrub with large wine-red, water-lily-shaped flowers and aromatic foliage and bark, fragrant and adaptable for sun or part shade.
Southern catalpa is a spreading shade tree with huge heart-shaped leaves and showy white bell flowers spotted purple and yellow, followed by long bean-like pods. Host to the catalpa sphinx moth.
Northern catalpa is a fast-growing shade tree with bold heart-shaped leaves, large showy white flowers, and dangling cigar-like seed pods. Tough and adaptable; host to the catalpa sphinx moth.
Port Orford cedar is a tall, graceful Pacific Northwest conifer with soft, fern-like sprays of scale foliage and a spire-like crown. A stately native evergreen for moist, cool sites.
Atlantic white cedar is a slender wetland conifer of acidic swamps along the East Coast, with blue-green scale foliage. The sole larval host for the rare Hessel's hairstreak butterfly.
Satinleaf is a striking South Florida hammock tree whose leaves are glossy green above and shimmering coppery-gold beneath, with small fragrant flowers and dark edible fruit relished by birds.

American yellowwood is a refined native shade tree with smooth gray bark, bright green compound leaves turning gold in fall, and dramatic drooping panicles of fragrant white pea flowers. A nitrogen-fixing bee tree.
Buckwheat tree (titi) is an evergreen shrub or small tree of Southeastern bogs and streamheads, smothered in fragrant white-pink flower spikes that are a major early-season honeybee plant.

Silverberry (wolf-willow) is the native silver-leaved Elaeagnus of northern and western North America, with intensely fragrant yellow flowers and silvery edible berries. A nitrogen-fixer for dry, cold sites.
Eastern swampprivet is a wetland shrub or small tree of floodplains and swamps, with early greenish flowers and dark fruit that ducks and songbirds eat. A useful native for wet, shaded ground.
Florida swampprivet (Florida privet) is a dense, fine-textured coastal shrub with small early flowers and dark berries for birds, salt- and drought-tolerant and excellent for hedging.
Mountain witch alder is an Appalachian shrub with fragrant white bottlebrush flowers in spring and brilliant orange-red-purple fall color, a tidy, four-season native for sun or part shade.
Bowman's root is an airy woodland perennial with starry white flushed-pink flowers dancing on wiry red stems above three-parted leaves, with good fall color. Elegant and trouble-free for part shade.
Two-wing silverbell is a Southeastern understory tree that drips clusters of pure-white bell flowers along its branches in spring, followed by curious two-winged fruits. Lovely at the woodland edge.
Tecate cypress is a rare fire-adapted cypress of Southern California chaparral, with cherry-red bark and dense gray-green foliage; its cones open after fire. A tough evergreen for dry slopes.
Monterey cypress is the iconic wind-sculpted conifer of the California coast, with rich green foliage and a broad, characterful crown. Salt- and wind-tolerant for coastal plantings.
Yellow anise tree is a dense, fast evergreen shrub with aromatic, anise-scented olive-green leaves and small yellow flowers, superb for shade screening in moist Southeastern gardens.
Sierra laurel is a glossy evergreen mountain shrub of wet meadows and streambanks in the Pacific states, with upright clusters of white urn-shaped flowers. A neat native for moist, acidic shade.
Barbados cherry (wild crapemyrtle) is a south Texas shrub with delicate pink flowers much of the year and tart, vitamin-C-rich red cherries loved by birds and people. Drought-tolerant and ornamental.

Devilwood (wild olive) is a Southeastern evergreen large shrub or small tree with leathery leaves and intensely fragrant tiny white spring flowers, followed by dark fruit for birds. Tough and refined.
Scentless mock orange is a Southeastern shrub with arching branches covered in large, crisp white four-petaled flowers in late spring, an easy, graceful native for sun or part shade.
Fevertree is a rare Southeastern wetland small tree whose flowers are surrounded by big showy rose-pink petal-like bracts, giving a poinsettia-like display. A striking native for moist, acidic edges.
Water elm (planertree) is a small, fast-growing elm relative of Southern swamps and floodplains, tolerating prolonged flooding; its warty fruits feed wood ducks and other wildlife.
Mountain camellia is a prized Appalachian understory shrub or small tree with large, ruffled white camellia-like flowers with showy purple stamens in summer, plus brilliant fall color.
California snowbell (drug snowbell) is a chaparral and foothill shrub that hangs fragrant, bell-shaped white flowers along its branches in spring, drought-tolerant for dry, sunny California gardens.

Florida yew is a rare, shade-loving evergreen confined to ravines along Florida's Apalachicola River, with soft dark needles and red fleshy arils. A critically imperiled relict conifer; foliage and seeds are toxic.

Florida torreya (stinking cedar) is one of North America's rarest conifers, a ravine evergreen of the Apalachicola bluffs with stiff, pungent dark needles. Critically endangered and the focus of conservation efforts.
Honeycup is a Southeastern bog shrub with powdery blue-green foliage and dangling clusters of fragrant, anise-scented white bell flowers, a choice, refined native for moist, acidic gardens.
Columbian monkshood is a tall western mountain perennial with deeply cut leaves and hooded violet-blue flowers along wet streambanks and meadows. Dramatic for moist shade; all parts are highly toxic.
Coast rock cress is a compact California rosette plant that erupts in fragrant rose-purple flowers in early spring, a cheerful, drought-tolerant choice for sunny rock gardens and walls.
Silverweed is a creeping perennial of moist shores and meadows, with ferny silver-backed leaves and bright yellow buttercup-like flowers on runners. Spreads as a tough groundcover; roots are edible.
Sea thrift forms tidy grassy evergreen cushions topped with round pink-to-white flower pompons over a long season, a salt- and drought-tolerant native for coastal beds, rock gardens, and edging.
Doubting mariposa lily is a Southwestern bulb that lifts elegant cupped white-to-pinkish flowers, intricately marked within, on slender stems above grassy uplands. A drought-adapted summer gem.
Sego lily, Utah's state flower, is a Great Basin bulb with chalice-shaped white flowers brushed yellow and purple at the base. A drought-hardy beauty of dry grasslands and sagebrush.
Arctic bellflower is a diminutive alpine perennial of high, cold scree, holding a single nodding blue-violet bell on a wiry stem. A choice miniature for trough and rock gardens.

Fireweed is a bold pioneer perennial that paints burned and disturbed ground with spires of magenta-pink flowers, a major nectar source for bees (a prized honey plant) and hummingbirds. Spreads readily.
Dwarf fireweed (river beauty) is a low alpine and arctic perennial of gravelly riverbars, with large rose-pink flowers outsized for the plant. A choice, hardy native for cold rock gardens.
Soap plant (amole) is a California bulb sending up a tall, airy candelabra of white evening-opening flowers above a basal rosette of wavy leaves. Drought-adapted; the bulb was long used as soap.
Umbrella plant is a striking streamside perennial of the Pacific mountains that thrusts up pink flower clusters before its huge round parasol leaves emerge. Bold for pondsides and wet shade.

Blue dicks is a widespread western bulb that lifts dense clusters of blue-violet flowers on slender stalks in spring, a drought-adapted, easy native and an important early nectar source. Corms are edible.
Mountain avens is a mat-forming alpine evergreen subshrub with crinkled leaves, eight-petaled creamy flowers, and feathery silver seedheads. A nitrogen-fixing classic for limestone rock gardens.
Rockfringe is a low alpine mat smothered in surprisingly large rose-pink flowers in late summer, a showy, drought-tolerant native for sunny scree and rock-garden crevices.
Siskiyou willowherb is a rare serpentine-slope perennial of the Klamath region with blue-green leaves and large rose-pink flowers, a tough, drought-tolerant rock-garden plant for hot, lean soils.
Humboldt County fuchsia is a low, soft gray-felted California perennial that glows with scarlet-orange tubular flowers in late summer, a hummingbird magnet and drought-tolerant groundcover.
Prairie smoke is a beloved prairie perennial whose nodding dusky-pink flowers give way to wispy, smoke-like feathered seedheads that catch the light. Drought-tolerant and enchanting in masses.
Desert lily is a spectacular deep-set bulb of the Sonoran and Mojave deserts that sends up fragrant, lily-like white flowers after winter rains. A drought-dormant jewel of sandy flats.
Kalmiopsis is a rare, exquisite dwarf evergreen shrublet endemic to southwest Oregon, covered in rose-purple laurel-like flowers in spring. A prized, slow alpine for acidic, gritty rock gardens.

Cliff maids form succulent evergreen rosettes that throw up sprays of candy-striped flowers in pink, peach, white, and orange. A dazzling, drought-tolerant native for crevices and walls with sharp drainage.

Bitterroot, Montana's state flower, opens improbably large satiny rose-pink flowers nearly at ground level after its leaves wither. A drought-dormant treasure of dry, gravelly western slopes; roots were a food staple.

Partridgefoot is a low evergreen mat of finely dissected leaves topped with short spikes of creamy flowers, carpeting moist alpine meadows and slopes. A fine groundcover for cool, gritty mountain gardens.
Alpine sandwort builds dense emerald cushions studded with tiny white star flowers, hugging high, windswept fellfields. A choice, durable miniature for troughs and crevice gardens.
Woodland beardtongue is a graceful penstemon relative of moist Pacific Northwest forests, dangling rose-purple tubular flowers in open clusters. A hummingbird favorite for cool, part-shade gardens.

Mountain sorrel is a circumpolar alpine perennial with kidney-shaped leaves and reddish-green flower spikes that flush crimson in seed. The tangy, vitamin-C-rich leaves are edible; tidy for cool rock gardens.
Pink mountain heather is a low evergreen heath of high western slopes, forming needle-leaved mats hung with rosy bell flowers near melting snow. A choice, slow shrublet for cool, acidic rock gardens.

Slender cinquefoil is a graceful western meadow perennial with palmate leaves and airy sprays of bright yellow flowers all summer, an easy, drought-tolerant pollinator plant for sunny borders and prairies.
Common cinquefoil is a low, running eastern woodland perennial with strawberry-like leaves and small yellow five-petaled flowers in spring, a tough native groundcover for sun or part shade.
Scarlet cinquefoil is a Southwestern mountain perennial unusual for its deep wine-red flowers over a long summer season, a drought-tolerant, long-blooming native for sunny borders.
Spotted saxifrage forms tight, spiny evergreen mats of tiny rosettes topped with white flowers freckled red and yellow, a hardy, drought-tolerant native for crevices, troughs, and rock walls.

Purple mountain saxifrage is one of the earliest and hardiest of all alpines, pressing magenta flowers flat against trailing mats as snow recedes. A treasured, cold-loving plant for limestone crevices.

Three-toothed cinquefoil is a low, woody-based native with glossy three-toothed leaves that redden in fall and clusters of small white flowers, a tough evergreen-ish mat for sunny, acidic rocky ground.

Piggyback plant is a Pacific Northwest woodlander famous for sprouting new plantlets where leaf meets stalk, with spikes of slender chocolate-and-green flowers. A lush, easy groundcover for moist shade.
American globeflower is a rare buttercup relative of cold wet meadows and fens, holding cupped creamy-yellow flowers on slender stems in spring. A choice native for consistently moist, sunny ground.

Golden inside-out flower is a Siskiyou woodland perennial with leathery evergreen leaflets and airy sprays of nodding yellow flowers whose petals sweep backward. A refined evergreen groundcover for shade.
Modesty (yerba de selva) is a trailing Pacific-coast subshrub that mats the forest floor with small white flower clusters in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant evergreen groundcover for dry shade.
Beargrass is a dramatic western mountain perennial with a fountain of tough grasslike evergreen leaves and a towering creamy plume of tiny flowers. Fire-adapted and a striking, architectural native.

Snowball sand verbena is a sprawling Great Plains perennial whose rounded clusters of fragrant white trumpet flowers open in the evening to draw hawkmoths. A drought-proof native for deep, sandy soils.
Desert sand verbena is a low desert annual that carpets sandy flats with fragrant rose-magenta flower balls after winter rains, a beloved Southwestern wildflower for hot, sandy gardens.
Yellow Indian mallow is a soft-leaved Texas shrub with cheerful orange-yellow mallow flowers over a long warm season, drought-tolerant and a steady nectar source for bees and butterflies.
Huisache daisy is a fragrant Texas annual that blankets roadsides and fields with golden, honey-scented daisies in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny wildflower meadows.
Leopardbane is a Southeastern arnica of moist pine savannas, sending up bright yellow daisies on slender stems above a basal rosette. A cheerful native for sunny to lightly shaded moist ground.
Heartleaf arnica is a western mountain woodland perennial that spreads into colonies of heart-shaped leaves topped with large golden daisies, brightening the dappled floor of conifer forests.
Hairyseed bahia is a silvery, drought-hardy Southwestern perennial dotted with bright yellow daisies through the warm season, a tough, low groundcover for hot, dry, rocky desert gardens.
Hooker's balsamroot is a low Great Basin perennial with ferny silvery leaves and large golden sunflowers on short stems in spring, a long-lived, deeply taprooted native for dry western slopes.
Florida greeneyes is a Florida endemic perennial that holds yellow daisies with distinctive green centers nearly year-round above a basal rosette, a tough, drought-tolerant native for sandy gardens.
Berlandier's sundrops is a low, long-blooming Southern Plains perennial smothered in clear yellow four-petaled flowers, a drought-proof, deer-resistant native for sunny borders and rock gardens.
Plains yellow primrose is a wiry, drought-tough perennial of the Great Plains and Rockies, dotted with small clear-yellow flowers all summer. An easy, deer-resistant native for hot, dry, lean soils.
Beach evening primrose is a silvery prostrate perennial that mats coastal dunes with bright yellow flowers much of the year, a superb salt- and drought-tolerant groundcover for sandy seaside gardens.
Cottony golden aster is a woolly, silver-felted Southeastern perennial that lights up sandhills and pinelands with golden asters in fall, a drought-tolerant native for sunny, sandy gardens.
Red ribbons is a California annual with extraordinary fringed, three-lobed deep-pink petals like tiny ribbons, an enchanting, easy reseeder for sunny to lightly shaded dry slopes.
Ruby chalice clarkia is a California coastal annual with bowl-shaped rose-pink flowers marked a deep red in the center, a cheerful, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny gardens and meadows.
Red spot clarkia is a low California annual with cupped pink-to-white petals each marked with a bright red spot, an easy, drought-tolerant wildflower for sunny, well-drained gardens.
Elegant clarkia is a tall, slender California annual with whimsical diamond-shaped clawed petals in pink to lavender along wand-like stems, a graceful, drought-tolerant reseeder and good cut flower.
Chinese houses is a California annual whose tiered whorls of bicolored white-and-purple flowers stack like a pagoda, a charming, easy reseeder for the dappled light of woodland edges.
Narrowleaf blue-eyed Mary is a delicate western annual with small blue-and-white snapdragon-like flowers, an airy, easy native for part shade in dry, open woodlands and slopes.
Grand collomia is an unusual western annual with clusters of soft salmon-apricot trumpet flowers atop leafy stems, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder with a rare and lovely flower color.
Tiny trumpet is a slender, widespread western annual with small pink-to-white tubular flowers in leafy clusters, a modest, drought-tolerant pioneer for dry, open, sandy ground.
California aster is a silvery, drought-tough perennial that hazes dry slopes with lavender-pink asters from summer into fall, a long-blooming, low-care native and valuable late-season nectar source.
Southwestern cosmos is an airy desert annual with feathery foliage and dainty pink-to-lavender daisies after summer rains, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny Southwestern gardens.
Clasping coneflower is a smooth, blue-green Southern annual with drooping yellow petals around a tall conical brown center, an easy reseeder that thrives in moist clay and seasonally wet ground.
Engelmann's daisy is a deep-rooted Great Plains perennial covered for months in lemon-yellow daisies that curl up in afternoon heat, a tough, drought- and clay-tolerant native for sunny meadows.
Silver fleabane is a silky, silver-leaved Great Basin perennial with lavender-pink daisies on tidy stems, a compact, drought-proof native for hot, dry rock gardens and gravelly slopes.
Cutleaf daisy is a low, cushion-forming alpine and western perennial with finely dissected leaves and white-to-pink daisies, a charming, drought-tolerant miniature for troughs and rock gardens.
Spreading fleabane is a soft, much-branched western annual smothered for months in small white-to-lavender daisies, an easy, drought-tolerant pioneer and steady nectar plant for dry, open ground.

Philadelphia fleabane is a widespread native perennial with many small pink-rayed daisies in spring, an easy, adaptable wildflower for sunny to part-shaded moist meadows and an early pollinator favorite.
Robin's plantain is a soft-hairy eastern woodland perennial that spreads by runners into patches topped with large lavender-and-yellow daisies in spring, an easy native groundcover for part shade.
Oakleaf fleabane is a Southeastern perennial with lobed basal leaves and airy sprays of small white-to-lavender daisies in spring, an easy, adaptable native for sunny, sandy gardens and lawns.
Texas stork's bill is a low desert annual that opens rich magenta-purple flowers after winter rains, an early-season nectar source and cheerful, drought-adapted reseeder for sunny, gravelly ground.
Tufted poppy is a delicate California annual forming neat mounds of ferny blue-green leaves studded with bright yellow cups, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny banks and rock gardens.
Seaside gentian (catchfly prairie gentian) is the wild native lisianthus, with large satiny blue-purple bell flowers on blue-green stems, a stunning, long-blooming native and superb cut flower.
California gilia is a spring annual with lacy leaves and dense heads of violet-blue funnel flowers on slender stems, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that draws bees to sunny meadows and slopes.
Bird's eyes is a charming California annual whose small flowers combine a violet throat, yellow eye, and pale blue lobes, and smell faintly of chocolate. An easy, fragrant reseeder for sunny gardens.

Rose vervain is a sprawling, long-blooming native verbena that mats the ground with rounded clusters of rose-purple flowers from spring to fall, a tough, drought-tolerant butterfly magnet and groundcover.
Showy goldeneye is an airy western perennial that floats masses of small golden daisies on wiry stems through summer and fall, a drought-tolerant, long-blooming native for sunny meadows and slopes.
Scorpionstail is a warm-climate perennial with curled spikes of tiny white flowers that uncoil as they bloom nearly year-round, a tough, drought-tolerant nectar source and host for some butterflies.
Seaside heliotrope is a fleshy, blue-green native of saline flats and shores, with coiled spikes of small white-and-blue flowers, a salt-tolerant, drought-hardy groundcover for tough, alkaline sites.

Sharsmith's western flax is a rare, threadlike serpentine-endemic annual of California's inner Coast Ranges, with tiny pale flowers. A specialized collector's native for sharply drained, lean, rocky soils.
Cooper's rubberweed is a tidy western perennial bearing bright yellow daisies on slender stems above a leafy base, a drought-proof, deer-resistant native for hot, dry, rocky slopes and gardens.
Pingue rubberweed is a low, mounding Great Plains and Rocky Mountain perennial smothered in golden daisies over a long season, an exceptionally drought- and cold-tough native for lean, sunny ground.

Streambank wild hollyhock is a stately western perennial with maple-like leaves and spires of soft pink mallow flowers, a striking, fire-following native for moist meadows, streambanks, and sunny borders.
Scarlet gilia (skyrocket) is a western biennial that sends up wands of trumpet-shaped scarlet flowers irresistible to hummingbirds, a spectacular, drought-tolerant native for sunny, gravelly slopes.

Flaxflowered ipomopsis is an airy Southwestern annual with long, pale blue-white trumpet flowers on wispy stems, opening toward evening to draw moths. A delicate, drought-adapted reseeder for sandy ground.

Standing cypress is a dramatic biennial that rockets up a tall spire of fiery red tubular flowers loved by hummingbirds, with feathery foliage. A drought-tolerant, self-sowing showstopper for sunny gardens.
Thurber's gilia is a Southwestern annual with slender pink-red trumpet flowers after summer rains, a drought-adapted reseeder that brings hummingbirds to hot, sandy desert gardens.
Arizona poppy is a sprawling summer annual that carpets the desert with brilliant orange poppy-like flowers after monsoon rains, an easy, drought-adapted reseeder for hot, sunny Southwestern gardens.
California goldfields is a tiny spring annual that turns whole fields and slopes molten gold with masses of small daisies, a classic, easy reseeder for sunny meadows and a key early bee plant.

Yellowray goldfields is a vernal-pool annual that rings drying pool margins with sheets of golden daisies in spring, an easy reseeder for seasonally wet, sunny, alkaline ground and a valued bee plant.

White tidytips is a fragrant western annual with crisp white ray flowers around a yellow center, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that brightens sandy flats and deserts and feeds spring pollinators.

False babystars is a delicate California annual whose pinwheel flowers in pink, lilac, and white float on threadlike stems above whorled leaves, a dainty, easy reseeder for sunny to lightly shaded gardens.

Large-flowered leptosiphon is a California annual with showy clusters of pale pink-to-white trumpet flowers, fragrant in the evening, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny, sandy gardens and meadows.

Arizona bladderpod is a low, silvery perennial that hugs rocky desert ground with bright yellow flowers in spring and inflated little seed pods, a tough, drought-proof native for hot, gravelly slopes.
Gordon's bladderpod is a cheerful Southern Plains annual that spreads sheets of golden-yellow flowers across sandy ground in spring, an easy, drought-adapted reseeder and early-season nectar source.
Spreading bladderpod is a bright Texas annual with slender stems of clear yellow flowers in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that lights up roadsides and sunny, sandy wildflower gardens.

Douglas' meadowfoam is a low California annual of moist swales that foams with fragrant white-and-yellow flowers in spring, an exceptional bee plant and easy reseeder for sunny, seasonally wet ground.
Fringed linanthus is a tiny Southern California annual with outsized fringed pink flowers held just above the soil, a delightful, drought-adapted reseeder for sunny, sandy openings and rock gardens.
Texas star is a cheerful Texas annual bearing five-petaled golden-yellow daisies atop leafy stems in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny gardens, meadows, and tough clay soils.

Fringed puccoon is a deep-rooted prairie perennial with showy, ruffled trumpet-shaped pale-yellow flowers in spring, a long-lived, drought-proof native for sunny, well-drained prairies and rock gardens.

Bigelow's tansyaster is a Southwestern annual or biennial that crowns the season with violet-purple asters on bushy stems in late summer and fall, a drought-tolerant native and valuable late nectar source.

Common madia (tarweed) is an aromatic California annual whose yellow daisies, often red-centered, open in the cool of morning and evening, a long-blooming, drought-tolerant reseeder and good bee and seed plant.

Blazing star is a bold western perennial with large, glistening lemon-yellow flowers like starbursts that open in late afternoon, a striking, exceptionally drought-tolerant native for hot, dry, rocky ground.

Lindley's blazing star is a California annual with large, fragrant golden flowers that open toward evening, each with a starburst of stamens, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny, gravelly gardens.

Wild four o'clock is a tough, deep-rooted prairie perennial with heart-shaped leaves and small magenta flowers that open in late afternoon, a drought-proof native for sunny, dry meadows and roadsides.

Mountain coyote mint is an aromatic western perennial forming low mounds topped with lavender flower clusters that bees and butterflies swarm, a fragrant, drought-tolerant native for sunny rock gardens.
Basin nemophila is a delicate western annual of moist, shaded slopes with tiny pale blue-white flowers, a dainty, easy reseeder for cool, lightly shaded spots in the woodland or rock garden.
Five spot is a charming California annual whose cupped white flowers each bear a vivid purple blotch at the tip of every petal, an easy, fast reseeder for sun or part shade in cool-season gardens.
Small palafox is a wiry late-season annual of limestone glades and dry ground, dotted with small pink-lavender flowers, a drought-tolerant native that feeds fall pollinators in lean, rocky soils.
Coastal plain palafox is a Southeastern sandhill annual with airy clusters of rosy-purple flowers in fall, a drought-tolerant native that brightens dry, sandy gardens late in the season.

Rock rose (Texas swampmallow) is a rounded, soft-leaved subshrub that produces clear pink hibiscus-like flowers nearly nonstop through the warm season, a tough, drought-proof native for sunny Texas gardens.
Bolander's phacelia is a soft-haired coastal perennial of the Pacific Northwest with coiled clusters of lavender-blue bell flowers, an easy native for cool, part-shaded woodland edges and slopes.
Blue curls is a Texas annual whose coiled spikes of blue-violet flowers uncurl as they bloom, a superb spring bee plant and easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny wildflower gardens.

Wild Canterbury bells is a Southern California annual with showy, deep purple bell-shaped flowers, a striking, drought-tolerant reseeder that follows winter rains and feeds early bees in sunny gardens.
Parry's phacelia is a California annual covered in rich royal-purple, bowl-shaped flowers with pale centers, a dazzling, drought-tolerant reseeder and important early nectar source for sunny gardens.

Silky phacelia is a striking alpine perennial of the western mountains with silvery, ferny leaves and dense spikes of violet flowers studded with golden stamens, a choice native for sunny rock gardens.

Sticky phacelia is a California annual with intensely blue, white-centered flowers on sticky, aromatic stems, a vivid, drought-tolerant reseeder that draws bees to sunny, sandy gardens after winter rains.
White fiestaflower is a sprawling, shade-loving California annual that scrambles through brush with small white-to-bluish flowers, an easy reseeder for the cool, part-shaded floor of dry woodlands.

Cream cups is a low California annual with soft, hairy foliage and cupped pale-yellow-to-cream flowers on slender stems, a gentle, drought-tolerant reseeder that carpets sunny, sandy openings in spring.
Kiss-me-quick is a low, fleshy native purslane with bright magenta flowers that open in the heat of the day, a heat- and drought-proof groundcover for hot, sandy, sunny gardens and rock crevices.
Devil's claw is a bold, sticky annual with large orchid-like pink-and-cream flowers followed by curious hooked seed pods, a curiosity native of warm regions whose young pods are edible.

Woolly paperflower is a woolly, mounding Southwestern perennial smothered for months in papery golden daisies that dry and persist on the plant, an exceptionally drought-tolerant native for hot, dry gardens.
Gooseberryleaf globemallow is a silvery Great Basin perennial with spires of glowing apricot-orange mallow flowers, a tough, drought-proof native that lights up hot, dry, rocky western gardens.
Munro's globemallow is a long-blooming western perennial with soft gray-green leaves and abundant grenadine-orange cups, a heat- and drought-tolerant native for sunny borders and xeric gardens.
Smallflower globemallow is a drought-hardy Southwestern perennial with silvery foliage and many small orange mallow flowers over a long season, a tough, cheerful native for hot, dry, rocky ground.

Desert prince's plume is a dramatic western perennial that sends up tall, feathery spires of bright yellow flowers above blue-green leaves, a striking, drought-proof native for hot, dry, alkaline soils.

Wind poppy is a California annual with satiny brick-orange to red flowers marked deep red at the center, dancing on slender stems, an enchanting, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny, well-drained gardens.
Sidebeak pencilflower is a low, wiry native legume of dry eastern pinelands and prairies, dotted with small orange-yellow pea flowers through summer, a nitrogen-fixing, drought-tolerant ground plant.

Stemless four-nerve daisy is a tidy, long-lived perennial that lifts bright yellow daisies on bare stalks above tufts of narrow leaves, an exceptionally tough, drought- and cold-hardy native for rock gardens.

Four-nerve daisy is a Southern Plains perennial that blooms golden daisies on slender stalks nearly year-round, a deer-resistant, drought-proof native for sunny borders, rock gardens, and tough soils.

Stiff greenthread is an airy, ferny-leaved annual that floats golden, brown-centered daisies on wiry stems for months, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that brightens sunny, dry meadows and roadsides.

Fiveneedle pricklyleaf (dogweed) is a tiny, aromatic Southwestern perennial that mounds with bright golden daisies almost year-round, a heat- and drought-proof native for hot, dry rock gardens and edging.

Stemless Townsend daisy (Easter daisy) is a low cushion perennial of the western plains that presses large white-to-pink daisies flat against the ground in early spring, a charming, hardy rock-garden gem.

Mule's ears is a bold western perennial with large, glossy, resinous leaves and big sunflower-like blooms in late spring, a long-lived, drought-tolerant native that forms striking clumps in sunny meadows.
Narrowleaf mule's ears is a California perennial with narrow leaves and large golden sunflowers in spring, a tough, deep-rooted native that brightens sunny grasslands and meadows after the rains.
Seaside joyweed is a low, fleshy, salt-tolerant native that mats coastal dunes and shores with small white flower clusters, a tough groundcover for hot, sandy, seaside gardens.
American beachgrass is the premier dune-building grass of the Atlantic and Great Lakes coasts, its deep, spreading rhizomes trapping and stabilizing windblown sand. Essential for coastal restoration.
Alpine pussytoes is a low, silvery mat-forming perennial of high mountains, with woolly leaves and tufted white flower heads, a tough groundcover and larval host for American lady butterflies.

Plantainleaf pussytoes is a spreading native groundcover with broad, silvery basal leaves and fuzzy white flower clusters in spring, a tough, drought-tolerant lawn alternative and larval host for American lady butterflies.

Spreading dogbane is a bushy native perennial with drooping clusters of fragrant pink bell flowers that butterflies adore, a milkweed relative for sunny, dry ground. Spreads by rhizomes; sap is toxic.

Ovateleaf Indian plantain is a tall Southeastern perennial of moist pinelands and savannas, with bold basal leaves and flat clusters of small whitish flowers that draw many pollinators to wet, sunny ground.

Coastalplain honeycombhead is a Southeastern sandhill biennial that branches into a cloud of golden daisies in fall, with a distinctive honeycombed seed head, a drought-tolerant native for hot, sandy gardens.

Smooth beggarticks (bur marigold) is a wetland perennial that blazes with large golden daisies in fall along pond edges and marshes, a showy, easy native for consistently wet, sunny ground and a fine late bee plant.

Vanillaleaf is a Southeastern perennial whose dried leaves smell of vanilla, holding flat clusters of rosy-purple flowers above pine-savanna grass in fall, a fragrant, drought-tolerant native for sunny, sandy gardens.

Yellow thistle is a bold native thistle with large, spiny-collared yellow (sometimes pink-purple) flower heads in spring, a magnet for bees and butterflies and a seed source for goldfinches. A dramatic, deer-proof native.
Scarlet calamint is an aromatic Southeastern subshrub that flashes brilliant red tubular flowers for much of the year, a hummingbird favorite and drought-tolerant native for sunny, sandy gardens.
Yerba buena is a trailing, mat-forming Pacific-coast perennial with fragrant, mint-scented leaves long brewed as tea and tiny white flowers, an easy, aromatic native groundcover for cool part shade.

Birdbill dayflower is a Southwestern perennial with grassy leaves and vivid deep-blue three-petaled flowers that open fresh each morning after summer rains, a charming, drought-tolerant native for sunny gardens.
Whitemouth dayflower is a widespread native with clear blue, two-eared flowers opening each morning over a long season, an easy, drought-tolerant perennial for sunny to lightly shaded sandy gardens.
False rosemary is an aromatic, needle-leaved Southeastern shrub that mounds with pale lavender flowers in spring, a fragrant, drought- and salt-tolerant native for hot, sandy, sunny gardens.
Subalpine larkspur is a robust Rocky Mountain perennial with tall spires of deep blue-purple flowers in high meadows, a striking native for moist, sunny mountain gardens. All parts are toxic.

Little larkspur is a compact northern Rockies and Plains perennial with rich blue-and-white flowers in spring, an early, drought-tolerant native for sunny, dry meadows and rock gardens. All parts are toxic.

California larkspur is a stout perennial of California woodlands and chaparral, sending up tall stalks of pale purple-white flowers in spring, a bold native for sunny to lightly shaded gardens. All parts are toxic.

Scarlet larkspur is a spectacular California perennial that rockets up wands of brilliant red flowers irresistible to hummingbirds, a drought-tolerant, summer-dormant native for hot, sunny slopes. All parts are toxic.

Carolina larkspur is a slender prairie and glade perennial with spikes of blue to white flowers in late spring, a graceful, drought-tolerant native for sunny meadows across the central and southern states. All parts are toxic.
Tall larkspur is a rare Appalachian perennial that lifts elegant spires of blue-violet flowers in summer above rich woodland soil, a choice native for moist part shade. All parts are toxic.

Rockslide larkspur is a low alpine perennial of Pacific Northwest scree and talus, with stout stems of deep blue-purple flowers, a choice, drought-tolerant native for sunny rock gardens. All parts are toxic.
Sierra larkspur is a towering western perennial of wet mountain meadows and streambanks, with long spires of blue-purple flowers, a dramatic native for moist, sunny ground. All parts are toxic.

Twolobe larkspur is a dainty western foothill perennial that opens deep blue-purple flowers in early spring before going summer-dormant, an easy, drought-tolerant native for sunny, dry gardens. All parts are toxic.

Desert larkspur is a Mojave and Great Basin perennial with sky-blue flowers on slender stems after spring rains, a drought-adapted, summer-dormant native for hot, dry, sandy gardens. All parts are toxic.

Barestem larkspur is a Southwestern perennial that holds deep blue-purple flowers on nearly leafless stalks above a basal rosette in spring, a tidy, drought-tolerant native for sunny desert gardens. All parts are toxic.

Dwarf larkspur is a beloved spring-ephemeral woodland perennial of the eastern states, carpeting rich forests with blue, purple, and white flowers before going dormant, a choice native for part shade. All parts are toxic.

Royal larkspur is a California grassland perennial with unusually large, deep royal-purple flowers in spring, a showy, drought-tolerant, summer-dormant native for sunny meadows and gardens. All parts are toxic.

Illinois bundleflower is a ferny-leaved prairie legume with round white puffball flowers and curious clustered seed pods, a nitrogen-fixing native that feeds pollinators and provides high-protein seed for wildlife.

Oblongleaf snakeherb is a low Southeastern perennial that spreads into a groundcover dotted with lavender-blue flowers much of the year, an easy, drought-tolerant native and larval host for buckeye butterflies.

Trailing arbutus is a cherished, slow-growing native groundcover of acidic eastern woodlands, pressing intensely fragrant pink-white flowers among leathery evergreen leaves in earliest spring. A choice plant for cool, shaded, acidic soil.

Golden creeper is a sprawling, salt-tolerant coastal shrub of South Florida with glossy leaves, small pink-white flowers, and golden berries, an excellent drought-proof groundcover for hot, sandy seaside gardens.

Candelilla is a striking, nearly leafless Chihuahuan Desert shrub of slender, blue-green wax-coated stems, tipped with tiny pink-white flowers, an architectural, exceptionally drought-proof native of West Texas.

Plain (cream) gentian is a prairie and woodland-edge perennial with clusters of creamy bottle-shaped flowers in late summer, pollinated by strong bumblebees that force their way in. A choice native for moist, sunny ground.

Bottle (closed) gentian holds deep blue-violet flowers that never fully open, sealed for the bumblebees strong enough to push inside, blooming in moist meadows and woods in fall. A treasured late-season native.

Soapwort gentian bears clusters of blue, nearly closed bottle flowers in fall along moist meadows and streambanks, a beautiful, bumblebee-pollinated native for consistently moist, sunny to part-shaded ground.
Utah sweetvetch is a hardy western legume with spikes of fragrant rose-purple pea flowers above ferny leaves, a nitrogen-fixing, drought-tolerant native for sunny, dry slopes; its roots are edible.
Bitterweed is a fine-leaved annual that froths with small yellow daisies from summer into fall, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder for sunny, sandy ground and a steady late-season nectar source.

Bigelow's sneezeweed is a western perennial of wet meadows with bold golden daisies around a dark domed center, a showy, long-blooming native for consistently moist, sunny ground and a fine cut flower.
Azure bluet is a dainty native perennial that sprinkles lawns and woodland edges with tiny pale-blue, yellow-eyed flowers in spring, a charming, easy groundcover for moist, lightly shaded ground.

Longleaf bluet is a slender native perennial of dry, rocky openings with airy clusters of small white-to-lilac flowers in early summer, a tough, drought-tolerant wildflower for sunny to part-shaded lean soils.
Roundpod St. John's wort is a Southeastern wetland perennial with bright yellow flowers and reddish stems, an easy, sun-loving native for moist to wet, sandy, acidic ground around pond and bog edges.
Pineweed (orange grass) is a wiry, nearly leafless annual that dots dry, sandy ground with minuscule yellow flowers, turning rusty orange in fall, an easy native pioneer for hot, lean, sunny sites.

St. Andrew's cross is a low native subshrub whose pale yellow flowers form a distinctive four-petaled cross, blooming through summer in dry woodlands and sandy openings. Tough and drought-tolerant for sun or part shade.

Fourpetal St. John's wort is a Florida wetland shrub with clasping blue-green leaves and bright yellow four-petaled flowers much of the year, a tough, sun-loving native for moist to wet, sandy, acidic ground.

Western Labrador tea is an aromatic evergreen shrub of western mountain bogs and streamsides, with leathery leaves and flat clusters of white flowers, a fragrant native for cool, moist, acidic ground. Foliage is toxic.
Labrador tea is a northern bog shrub with fragrant, leathery evergreen leaves (long steeped as a tea) and rounded clusters of white flowers, a classic native for cold, wet, acidic, peaty ground.

Roundhead lespedeza is an upright prairie legume with silvery leaves and dense cream flower heads ripening to persistent rusty seed clusters, a nitrogen-fixing native that feeds pollinators and provides winter seed for birds.

Twinflower is a delicate, circumboreal evergreen groundcover that carpets cool conifer forests and dangles paired, fragrant pink bell flowers on threadlike stems. A beloved, dainty native for moist, acidic shade.

Berlandier's yellow flax is a Southern Plains annual with cup-shaped golden-orange flowers, often red-centered, on slender stems, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that brightens sunny, sandy gardens in spring.
Texas flax is a wiry annual that scatters small bright yellow flowers across dry, sandy ground in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant native reseeder for sunny, lean Southwestern gardens.
King's flax is a tufted Great Basin and Rocky Mountain perennial with clear yellow flowers on slender stems, a tough, drought- and cold-hardy native for sunny, rocky mountain gardens.
New Mexico yellow flax is a slender Southwestern mountain perennial with delicate golden flowers over a long summer season, a drought-tolerant native for sunny, well-drained, rocky gardens.
Meadow flax is a Southern Plains annual with sky-blue, satiny flowers that open fresh each morning in spring, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder that drifts beautifully through sunny, sandy meadows.
Stiffstem flax is a wiry Great Plains annual with bright yellow, often orange- or red-centered flowers, an easy, drought-tolerant native reseeder for sunny, dry, lean prairies and gardens.
Deerweed is a fast, broom-like California subshrub covered in small yellow pea flowers that age orange-red, a key nitrogen-fixing fire-follower, larval host, and bee plant for hot, dry, sunny slopes.

Grassleaf Barbara's buttons is a Southeastern perennial of moist savannas with grassy leaves and pom-pom heads of fragrant pink-lavender flowers in summer, a refined native for sunny, moist, sandy ground.

Woolly broomwood (teabush) is a soft-felted subtropical shrub of far South Texas and Florida with rosy-lavender flowers nearly year-round, a drought- and heat-tolerant native nectar plant for hot, sunny gardens.

Redwood sorrel is a lush, clover-leaved groundcover that carpets the floor of Pacific coast forests and folds its leaves in bright light, with dainty pink-white flowers. A superb native for moist, acidic deep shade.

Common yellow woodsorrel is a widespread native with clover-like leaves and small bright yellow flowers all season, an easy, adaptable wildflower for sun or part shade; its tangy leaves and pods are edible.

Mariola is a low, silvery-leaved Chihuahuan Desert shrub dotted with tiny cream flowers over a long season, an exceptionally drought- and heat-tolerant native for hot, dry, rocky Southwestern gardens.

Wild quinine is a sturdy prairie perennial with coarse leaves and flat clusters of pearly white flowers all summer, a tough, drought-tolerant native that supports many pollinators and makes a long-lasting cut flower.

Bladderpod is a rounded, gray-green California desert shrub that blooms bright yellow flowers nearly year-round, followed by inflated pods, a major bee and pollinator plant and a tough, drought-proof evergreen.
Jones' beeplant is a Colorado Plateau annual with palmate leaves and clusters of yellow flowers in early summer, a drought-adapted native and excellent bee plant for hot, dry, rocky western gardens.
Yellow beeplant is a Great Basin annual that lifts showy heads of golden flowers on branching stems, a superb, drought-tolerant nectar and pollen source for native bees in sunny, dry western gardens.

Slender spiderflower is a low perennial of alkaline flats and shores in Texas and the Southwest, with airy pink-white flowers much of the year, a salt- and drought-tolerant native bee plant for tough soils.

Golden beeplant is a Great Basin and Rocky Mountain annual with clusters of bright yellow flowers and flat seed pods, a drought-adapted, sticky-aromatic native and valuable bee plant for hot, dry western ground.

Rocky Mountain beeplant is a tall western annual with showy clusters of rose-purple flowers all summer, one of the very best native bee and pollinator plants for sunny, dry, sandy gardens. An easy reseeder.
Pitted stripeseed is a low Florida perennial that opens cheerful yellow flowers nearly year-round on wiry stems, a drought-tolerant native for hot, sunny, sandy gardens and a steady nectar source.

Selfheal is a low, spreading native (subsp. lanceolata) with squat spikes of violet flowers all summer, an easy, tough groundcover and bee plant for sun or part shade; long valued as a medicinal herb.
Blackroot is a silvery-leaved Southeastern perennial of dry pinelands with dense white flower spikes like a bottlebrush, a drought-tolerant native for hot, sunny, sandy ground.

California buttercup is a cheerful native perennial that spangles spring grasslands and oak woodlands with glossy golden flowers, an easy, drought-deciduous wildflower for sun or part shade. Toxic if eaten.
Heartleaf buttercup is a western mountain perennial with glossy yellow flowers above heart-shaped basal leaves, a hardy native for moist, sunny meadows and streamsides. Toxic if eaten.

Alkali buttercup is a low, runnering perennial of wet shores and alkaline flats, with small yellow flowers and scalloped leaves, a tough, salt-tolerant native groundcover for seasonally wet, sunny ground.
Needle palm is a clumping, fan-leaved native palm of Southeastern river bluffs — the most cold-hardy palm in the world — forming a lush evergreen mound for moist shade. Sharp needles arm its crown.

Pigeonberry is a graceful shade perennial that bears slender spikes of white-pink flowers and glossy red berries at the same time nearly year-round, a superb native groundcover for shade that feeds songbirds.

Carolina wild petunia is an easygoing native perennial with lavender-blue trumpet flowers all summer, a tough, drought-tolerant plant for sun or part shade and a larval host for common buckeye butterflies.

Nightflowering wild petunia is a rare Southeastern perennial of moist pine savannas whose large, fragrant white trumpet flowers open at dusk for hawkmoths, a choice native for sunny, moist, sandy ground.

Violet wild petunia is a drought-tough native perennial that opens fresh lavender-violet trumpets each morning through the warm season, an easy plant for sunny, dry gardens and a larval host for buckeye butterflies.

Beach naupaka (inkberry) is a fleshy, salt- and drought-proof native shrub of South Florida dunes, with fan-shaped white flowers and black fruit for birds. An excellent evergreen stabilizer for hot, sandy coasts.
Helmet skullcap is a slender native perennial topped with showy hooded blue-violet flowers in early summer, an easy, drought-tolerant plant for sunny to part-shaded gardens and a good bee plant.
Heartleaf skullcap is a soft, silvery-leaved woodland perennial with spikes of hooded blue-violet flowers in summer, a handsome, drought-tolerant native groundcover for part shade.

Lindheimer's senna is a velvety-leaved Texas perennial with spikes of golden flowers in late summer, a drought-tolerant native legume that is a larval host for cloudless sulphur and other sulphur butterflies.
Two-leaf senna is a low, mounding Southwestern perennial covered for months in bright yellow flowers, a drought-proof native legume and larval host for sulphur butterflies in hot, dry, rocky gardens.

Shrubby (Wislizenus') senna is a desert legume shrub that bursts into masses of golden flowers after summer rains, a striking, drought-proof native and larval host for sulphur butterflies in hot Southwestern gardens.

Cuban jute (arrowleaf sida) is a wiry native subshrub with small pale-yellow mallow flowers through the warm season, a tough, drought-tolerant plant for hot, sunny ground and a larval host for some skippers.
Moss campion is a tight, ancient alpine cushion that studs high, windswept fellfields with tiny pink flowers, an exquisite, exceptionally hardy native for troughs and crevice gardens.
Indian pink is a California woodland perennial that flares vivid scarlet, deeply fringed flowers irresistible to hummingbirds, a showy native for part shade with good drainage.
Carolina wild pink is a low eastern perennial that forms tufts topped with bright pink flowers in spring, a charming, drought-tolerant native for sunny to part-shaded rocky banks and rock gardens.
Hooker's catchfly is a low Pacific Northwest perennial with large, deeply lobed pink-to-salmon flowers nestled close to the ground in spring, a choice, drought-tolerant native for sunny rock gardens.

Royal catchfly is a stately prairie perennial that flares brilliant scarlet flowers in mid-summer, a spectacular hummingbird magnet and increasingly rare native for sunny, well-drained meadows and borders.
Diamondflowers (narrowleaf bluet) is a wiry native perennial dotted with small white-to-lavender flowers over a long season, a drought-proof plant for sunny, dry, rocky ground and rock gardens.

Goat's rue is a silky-leaved native legume of dry sandy ground with striking bicolored pink-and-cream pea flowers in early summer, a nitrogen-fixing, drought-proof plant and larval host for several butterflies.
American germander is an adaptable native perennial with spikes of lipped pink-lavender flowers in summer, a tough, spreading bee plant for sunny to part-shaded moist ground and pond edges.

Forked bluecurls is an aromatic native annual that opens jewel-like blue flowers with long arching stamens each morning in late summer, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder and fine bee plant for sandy, sunny ground.
Buffalo clover is a declining native clover with bicolored red-and-white flower heads in spring, a nitrogen-fixing legume that supports bees and is a conservation-worthy native for sunny meadows.
Golden crownbeard is a fast, gray-green native annual covered in bright yellow daisies through the warm season, an easy, drought-tolerant reseeder and steady nectar source for hot, dry, sunny ground.

Frostweed is a tall native perennial whose flat clusters of white flowers are a vital late-fall nectar source for migrating butterflies; on freezing mornings its split stems extrude ribbons of ice. Thrives in part shade.
Barren strawberry is a tidy, strawberry-like native groundcover with glossy semi-evergreen leaves and bright yellow spring flowers, an easy, drought-tolerant carpet for part to full shade.

Hercules' club (toothache tree) is a spiny native tree with aromatic citrus-scented foliage and corky, knobbed bark, a key larval host for giant swallowtail butterflies and a source of fruit for birds.

Lime pricklyash is a thorny, citrus-scented evergreen shrub of South Texas and Florida brush, a major larval host for giant swallowtail butterflies and cover and fruit for wildlife. Drought- and salt-tolerant.
